title: Silky Brazilian Chicken Stew
description: A delicious and refreshing Brazilian-inspired stew featuring herbal notes and a silky texture, perfect for a brunch gathering. This low-sodium dish showcases the brightness of unwaxed lemon and the richness of chicken braised in aromatic spices.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the chicken to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the dried oregano, salt-free Brazilian seasoning blend, and low-sodium chicken broth to the pot. Stir to combine, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in the coconut milk and unwaxed lemon juice. Continue to simmer, uncovered, for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the stew has thickened slightly.
Season the stew with salt to taste, then stir in the chopped fresh cilantro and parsley.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional cilantro and parsley if desired.
Let the stew cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
